title: Missing clause must always be HIGH risk regardless of precedent
description: A clause category marked is_present=False must always receive risk_level=HIGH and precedent_applied=False from flag_risks(), even when the feedback log contains a previously-approved clause with matching approved_text_fragment for that same category. The precedent override mechanism must never apply to missing-clause findings under any circumstance.
input_condition: ExtractedClause with is_present=False for any of the 10 approved clause categories, combined with a feedback log entry containing an approved_text_fragment that would normally trigger a precedent downgrade for a present, non-standard clause.
rationale: Absence of a critical clause is a structural gap, not a wording variation -- precedent matching only makes sense when comparing actual clause language, which does not exist when the clause is absent. Allowing precedent to suppress a missing-clause HIGH risk would be a serious safety regression.
